sections.paragraph:
ἀσύμφορος, ον (s. σύμφορος; Hes., Thu. et al.; Dio Chrys. 3, 91; 10 [11], 13; Pr 25:20; Philo) adj. ‘pert. to unsuitability’, then disadvantageous, harmful ἀ. ἐστί τινι it is harmful for someone 2 Cl 6:1; Hm 4, 3, 6; 5, 1, 4; 5, 2, 2; 6, 2, 6; Hs 1:10; w. inf. foll. 1:5.—Frisk s.v. φέρω.

Hes Hes , date uncertain, perh. before VI b.c.—List 5

Thu Thu , V b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

Dio Dio Chrys , I–II a.d.—List 5

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

adj. adj. = adjective

pert. pert. = pertaining (to)

2 Cl 2 Cl = 2 Clement—List 1

Hm Hm = Mandates

Hs Hs = Similitudes

inf. inf. = infinitive

foll. foll. = followed, following

Frisk Frisk = HF., Griechisches etymologisches Wörterbuch—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 146
